Horse Racing News

Cheltenham Festival 2025: Complete Ticket Buying Guide

ByTara Whitfield|15 February 2025  ·  7 min read

Cheltenham Festival draws over 250,000 racegoers across the four-day meeting. (Getty Images)

Everything you need to know about buying Cheltenham Festival tickets — from Champion Day to Gold Cup Day — and where to find the best prices.

Understanding the Four Days of the Festival

Cheltenham Festival spans four extraordinary days in March, each with its own identity. Champion Day (Tuesday) draws the largest crowds, headlined by the Champion Hurdle, and is consistently the most expensive day on the resale market. Ladies Day (Wednesday) blends fashion with serious racing. St Patrick's Thursday brings a festive Irish contingent and fierce demand for Arkle Chase tickets. Gold Cup Friday is the pinnacle — the Gold Cup itself is Europe's most prestigious steeplechase and hospitality packages for this day are often sold out 12 months in advance.

Official Versus Secondary Market: When to Choose Each

The Jockey Club releases official tickets in phases from October onward, and General Admission for Tuesday and Friday typically sells out within weeks. If you miss the official window, the secondary market becomes your only option. For General Admission on Wednesday and Thursday, prices on resale platforms often track only slightly above face value up to three weeks before the event, making late purchases viable. Grandstand Reserved and Club Enclosure tickets, however, command a steep premium whenever they appear secondhand — budget at least 60% above face value for peak days.

Hospitality Packages: Are They Worth the Investment?

Private boxes and hospitality suites at Cheltenham start at around £350 per person and can exceed £1,200 for Gold Cup Friday packages with a trackside view. For groups of four or more, the per-head cost of a hospitality package can actually compare favourably to buying premium grandstand seats individually on the secondary market while also including food, drink, and a dedicated betting concierge. The key is to book directly through official hospitality partners rather than third-party brokers who frequently add margins of 20% or more.

Practical Tips for Getting There and Making the Most of Your Day

Cheltenham Racecourse is located on the outskirts of Cheltenham town, roughly a 20-minute taxi ride from Cheltenham Spa rail station. Official coach services run from Birmingham New Street and London Victoria and are included in some ticket packages. Parking on-site is limited and must be pre-booked. Arrive at least 90 minutes before the first race to soak up the atmosphere, explore the parade ring, and queue for the best trackside positions in the General Admission areas.
